answer:
**In "Aaftab" (ch. 14 of *Bang-E-Dara Part 1*), the sun figures as the vital soul (*rooh-o-rawaan*) of the universe, the source of existence, consciousness, reason (*khirad*), and eternal life that transcends time.**[^1][^2] The poem directly apostrophizes the sun as the binder of time and space, the breath of life in all things, and the light (*noor*) of awareness (*shaoor*), urging it to bestow insight upon humanity.[^3]

**In "Mehr-e-Roshan" (ch. 32 of *Bang-E-Dara Part 1*), the "bright sun" (*mehr-e-roshan*) figures the transient light of worldly life that sets like the day, unveiling the darkness of death and prompting existential inquiry into the afterlife.**[^4] It hides (*chhip gaya*), lifting the veil from night's face and scattering evening's tresses over existence, as the poet addresses the dead ("sleepers in dust") about the mysteries beyond—wondering if sorrow, desire, and human limits persist there.[^5]

[^1]: Allama Iqbal, *Bang-E-Dara Part 1*, ch. 14 "Aaftab" (translation), [full chapter](/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-1-55b25c/14-aaftab/translation/).

[^2]: [p-96b3da] "O Sun! Thou art the world's own soul, its flowing grace, / Who binds the scattered leaves of all of Time and Space." Allama Iqbal, *Bang-E-Dara Part 1*, ch. 14 "Aaftab" (translation), [passage](/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-1-55b25c/14-aaftab/translation/#p-96b3da).

[^3]: [p-fdcce3] "That Sun, whose light makes all the world so bright, / Is Heart, is Mind, is Soul, is conscious Light." Allama Iqbal, *Bang-E-Dara Part 1*, ch. 14 "Aaftab" (translation), [passage](/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-1-55b25c/14-aaftab/translation/#p-fdcce3).

[^4]: Allama Iqbal, *Bang-E-Dara Part 1*, ch. 32 "Mehr-e-roshan" (translation), [full chapter](/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-1-55b25c/32-mehr-e-roshan/translation/).

[^5]: [p-202a06] "The shining sun has gone from sight, the veil is lifted from the face of night..." Allama Iqbal, *Bang-E-Dara Part 1*, ch. 32 "Mehr-e-roshan" (translation), [passage](/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-1-55b25c/32-mehr-e-roshan/translation/#p-202a06).
